title: Inspecting a network via the REST API does not work
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Inspecting a network via the REST API yields this seemingly unrelated error message 
```
getting container from store "9bef809f62af8fe14da2751b1560cf436281fba8b73f3e75d80172e7f6321f98": container not known
```
Inspecting networks via the CLI works as expected.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. get the ID of a network (for example the default `podman` network) via `podman network inspect`

2. request the same information via the REST API[^1]
```bash
curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http:/v1.32/networks/network-id-goes-here
```


**Describe the results you received:**
```json
{"cause":"container not known","message":"getting container from store \"9bef809f62af8fe14da2751b1560cf436281fba8b73f3e75d80172e7f6321f98\": container not known","response":500}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
no error

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
This happens with every network, even if it's not being used by any containers.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Fri Oct 21 10:16:35 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

The "container ID" from the error message is different from the network ID I requested. It's the same every time.

I encountered this trying to get the tests from https://github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-java working with podman.

[^1]: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/757fdca77b0fc72e61f7ee69f19a36144bfcac36/vendor/github.com/docker/docker/api/swagger.yaml#L9075-L9077

comments.body:
Sounds like your state is inconsistent. Somehow there is a mismatch between the podman boltdb and the container store.

Running `podman system reset` (will remove everything) should get it working again. Do you have a reproduce how you got into that situation?

> Sounds like your state is inconsistent. Somehow there is a mismatch between the podman boltdb and the container store.

That seems to be the issue. I debugged the request and the issue is a container that is present in boltdb but not the store.

During the conversion to a docker network, all containers are requested from boltdb and then inspected.
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/fbf441affd948e61d2833d05e735367eafcf4274/pkg/api/handlers/compat/networks.go#L68-L75

In my case, the offending container is the infra container of a pod. I remember having trouble with that pod being in an invalid state in the past, after `kube play --replace`.

Maybe the error could be handled more gracefully? For example, just skipping the container on `ErrContainerUnknown` and logging a warning.
How does this work with other podman commands or API calls? This problem does not look specific to this single endpoint.
@SoMuchForSubtlety do you have reproducer how you got into this state?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I can no longer reproduce this, so I think this can be closed.
